metadata.source: Per-region dossiers (legal.md / water.md / soil.md); contamination data was NOT a structured research focus, so the layer captures regulatory regime + known signals only
metadata.license: Research dossiers internal; underlying primary sources (national contamination registers) carry their own licenses
metadata.note: Carries red-line underlying data for soil-contamination-needing-5yr-remediation (Overview decision 9). V1 ships a thin honest version (regulatory regime + known signals); V2 would integrate national contamination registers (BASIAS/BASOL France, EPA Superfund US, Bundes-Bodenschutzgesetz Germany registers, etc.) for direct register hits per parcel.

properties.source: EASAC Groundwater in the Southern Member States of the European Union, Portugal (2020)
properties.gaps: Dossier does not address Portuguese soil-contamination regulatory regime, public contamination registers, or specific Alentejo contamination signals.

properties.known_contamination_notes: water.md flags aquifer pollution as a binding constraint alongside summer low-flow management but does not specify source or sector.
properties.gaps: No Spanish or Galician contamination regulatory regime cited, no register identified, no specific contamination drivers named.

properties.known_contamination_notes: water.md notes legacy agricultural and mining contamination in specific micro-watersheds, particularly downstream of former Communist-era industrial sites; recommends verifying via regional ANM groundwater monitoring network.
properties.gaps: Romanian contamination law not cited, no explicit public-register URL or system named beyond ANM monitoring.

properties.known_contamination_notes: water.md notes microbial contamination from poorly managed septic systems and peat-staining as primary private-supply quality issues; EPA Drinking Water Quality reports flag west-coast private supplies as country's main quality concern.
properties.source: EPA Ireland Drinking Water Quality reports as cited in Connemara water.md
properties.gaps: No Irish soil-contamination statute or public register cited; no industrial legacy signals explored.

properties.known_contamination_notes: water.md notes private supplies (~3% nationally, higher in rural Pembrokeshire) face contamination risk from agriculture and septic drainage per DWI Wales annual reports. No Milford Haven petrochemical signal in dossier.
properties.source: DWI Wales annual reports as cited in Pembrokeshire water.md
properties.gaps: No UK contaminated-land regime (Part 2A EPA 1990) cited, no public register, no explicit treatment of Milford Haven petrochemical legacy.
No French ICPE or BASOL/BASIAS register cited; no contamination signals discussed.
No Italian D.Lgs 152/2006 contamination regime cited; intensive apple-orchard pesticide signal not explicitly assessed in dossier.

properties.known_contamination_notes: water.md documents legacy mining contamination (coal, mercury, fluorite, lead) affecting Aller, Caudal, Nalon mid-reach sub-basins; IGME/CSIC and Confederacion Hidrografica del Cantabrico monitor and progressively remediate. Practitioner reading recommends full water-quality panel (heavy metals, sulfates, hydrocarbons) for parcels with historical mining in catchment. Soil.md confirms more legacy industrial contamination in central valleys than Galicia.
properties.source: Asturias water.md, citing IGME/CSIC and Confederacion Hidrografica del Cantabrico
properties.gaps: Spanish Real Decreto 9/2005 contaminated-soils regime not cited; no specific public-register URL provided.

properties.known_contamination_notes: water.md notes Elbe, Saale and Mulde surface-water quality remains affected by historical mining and industrial legacy. Dossier does not name Bitterfeld, Leuna, or specific ex-GDR contamination sites.
properties.gaps: German BBodSchG (Bundes-Bodenschutzgesetz) not cited, no Altlasten-Kataster reference, no parcel-level guidance for ex-GDR industrial heritage.

properties.known_contamination_notes: soil.md recommends due-diligence on Soviet-era contamination (former military or agricultural-collective sites) is worth scoping. water.md notes Saaremaa surface-water contamination from agriculture and saltwater intrusion risk near coast.
properties.gaps: Estonian environmental code not cited, no public contamination register named, no oil-shale-region treatment (northeast Estonia outside dossier scope).
Oregon DEQ contaminated-site program and Willamette Valley agricultural-chemical legacy not addressed in dossier.
Vermont DEC hazardous-sites list and any agricultural pesticide signals not addressed in dossier.
Coal-mining legacy (acid mine drainage), coal-ash impoundment, and TVA-region industrial heritage not addressed in dossier.

properties.known_contamination_notes: water.md documents karst-aquifer vulnerability with 15-20% of private wells in Pierce and Pepin counties exceeding EPA 10 mg/L nitrate limit; Wisconsin Groundwater Coordinating Council 2024 report flags nitrates, PFAS, and pesticides as primary statewide groundwater concerns with Driftless karst counties among highest-risk.
properties.source: Lake Pepin Legacy Alliance and Wisconsin Groundwater Coordinating Council as cited in Driftless water.md
properties.gaps: Wisconsin contaminated-land statutory framework not cited; well-by-well register access not documented.

properties.known_contamination_notes: water.md documents karst vulnerability: thin soil and fractured limestone allow rapid infiltration of surface pollutants; CAFO contamination (nitrates, E. coli) documented in northwest Arkansas karst systems near Benton/Washington counties per USGS science brief 2023. Site selection requires mapping contributing watershed not just parcel boundary.
properties.source: USGS science brief 2023 and TNC Ozark Highlands Karst Program as cited in Ozarks water.md
properties.gaps: Arkansas Department of Environmental Quality contaminated-sites register not named; no statutory regime cited.

properties.source: Northern New Mexico water.md, soil.md, legal.md
properties.gaps: Legacy uranium and molybdenum mining contamination across northern New Mexico, and Los Alamos/LANL plume not addressed in dossier.

properties.contamination_regulatory_regime: Water Resources Protection Act 2019 consolidated, predecessor Water Act
properties.known_contamination_notes: water.md flags agricultural nitrate contamination in Annapolis Valley lowlands, manganese and iron in some overburden aquifers, and historic-era acid deposition affecting lake pH in central Nova Scotia as primary quality risks.
properties.gaps: Statute named is water-resource law not a soil-contamination regime; no provincial contaminated-sites register cited.
Historic hardrock mining legacy in West Kootenay (Trail smelter, Sullivan mine catchment) and BC Site Registry not addressed in dossier.

properties.known_contamination_notes: water.md notes cyanobacteria blooms on Lac Brome and Lac Magog in warm summers driven by agricultural runoff (phosphorus from dairy-intensive farming); practitioner reading recommends well testing and proximity assessment to agricultural drains before purchase. Asbestos-mining legacy (regional history) not addressed in dossier.
properties.gaps: Quebec LQE (Loi sur la qualite de l'environnement) section IV.2.1 contaminated-sites regime and GTC public register not cited.

properties.known_contamination_notes: water.md notes waterborne contamination from upstream agriculture as a practical constraint in Sierra Norte and valley settlements; water use governed internally by ejido/comunal assembly rules rather than Conagua licences.
properties.gaps: Mexican LGEEPA contamination regime and any state-level Oaxaca register not cited; mining-district legacy (Sierra Norte historical mineral extraction) not addressed.
